title: FOSTER CARE OMBUDSMAN - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE FOSTER CARE OMBUDSMAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves as an independent resource to investigate complaints, concerns, or violations of rights for children in the custody of ODHS Foster Care. Established through legislation developed and passed by current and former foster youth, the office receives, investigates, and helps resolve concerns from a wide range of people, including foster youth, parents, relatives, CASAs, attorneys, social workers, and other interested parties. The Ombudsman listens to concerns, documents complaints, gathers all relevant information, remains neutral and impartial, and provides guidance on how to help. Operates the Youth Empowerment and Safety (YES) line, a hotline available not only for complaints but for foster youth to ask questions about their rights, care and well-being, or any issues with their placement or services while in care. 500 Summer Street NE E-17 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email - (855) 840-6036 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Toll-free (855) 840-6036 Youth Empowerment and Safety Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Advocacy * Former Foster Children Advocacy * Foster Children Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Birth Parents Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Former Foster Children Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Foster Children Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Foster Families Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Parents of Children in Foster Care Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER ASSISTANCE AND PROTECTION - GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE
